eu-etd.title: EU tobacco-tax revision (ETD)
eu-etd.summary: Would raise EU excise minima on cigars, cigarillos and pipe tobacco — STG's combustible core. This models the annual EBITDA at risk on the exposed share of the EU cigar/pipe base.
eu-etd.exposedBaseNote: EUB (Europe Branded) division net sales, used as the EU cigar/pipe base. STG does not publish an EU-excise-exposed revenue line, so the share actually below each new floor is the editable 'exposed share' assumption — not an STG figure.

assumptions.label: Exposed share of the EU base
assumptions.note: Share of the EU base sitting below the proposed new floor. Not stated in the source — you set it.

assumptions.label: Excise-driven retail price rise
assumptions.note: The directive sets minima, not a single headline rate — modeled as a scenario.

assumptions.label: Pass-through to the shelf
assumptions.note: Share of the excise that reaches the consumer vs STG absorbs. Drives the band.

assumptions.label: Contribution margin on lost volume
assumptions.note: STG does not disclose category contribution margin — an assumption.

assumptions.label: Own-price elasticity (cigars / pipe)
assumptions.note: No public elasticity consensus for cigars/pipe — a range you set. We deliberately do NOT show a cited cigarette elasticity against a cigar base. Drives the band.

facts.claim: EUB division net sales — the EU cigar/pipe base

abstain.claim: EU revenue sitting below each market's new excise floor
abstain.reason: not stated in source — needs human lookup (you set it via 'exposed share')

abstain.claim: The exact ETD rate increase for cigars / pipe
abstain.reason: COM(2025) 580 sets minima, not a single headline rate — modeled as a scenario, not quoted as fact

fr-ban.title: France oral-nicotine ban
fr-ban.appliesFrom: in force 1 Apr 2026 · Conseil d'État merits ruling ~Jun 2026
fr-ban.summary: France's total ban on oral nicotine (Décret 2025-898) removes the pouch category outright — there is no compliant product to recapture, so the affected revenue is lost, not deferred. The direct P&L hit is modest (pouches are ~5% of group), but the ban forecloses a market from STG's DKK 1bn+ pouch ambition. This models the foreclosed pouch revenue and its EBITDA on STG's published pouch base. The French machine-rolled cigar core (~DKK 620m, an analyst derivation) is a SEPARATE exposure — this ban does not touch it.
fr-ban.exposedBaseNote: NGP (nicotine-pouch) net sales FY2025 — STG's published pouch base (incl. XQS), ~5% of group. STG does not split it by market, so France's share is the editable assumption below, not an STG figure.

assumptions.label: France share of STG's pouch (NGP) base
assumptions.note: STG does not publish France pouch revenue — you set it. France was never an XQS focus market (Sweden/UK are), so the default is low.

assumptions.label: Share of the France pouch base hit by the ban
assumptions.note: A TOTAL ban on oral nicotine — only medicinal NRT is exempt — so this is ~100%.

assumptions.label: Revenue recaptured via compliant products
assumptions.note: There is no compliant oral-nicotine product in France post-ban — recapture is near zero. Drives the band.

assumptions.label: Contribution margin on lost pouch revenue
assumptions.note: STG does not disclose pouch contribution margin (NGP gross margin was 35.2% in Q1 2026, but gross ≠ contribution) — an assumption. Drives the band.

facts.claim: NGP (nicotine-pouch) net sales — the pouch base (FY2025)

abstain.claim: France pouch / oral-nicotine revenue for STG
abstain.reason: not published by STG — you set it via 'France share' (an analyst assumption, never STG's figure)
not stated in source — needs human lookup
no public consensus for pouches — contribution margin is an editable assumption, not a cited figure

dk-cap.title: Denmark 9 mg + flavour cap
dk-cap.appliesFrom: phased from 1 Jul 2025 · full effect 1 Apr 2026
dk-cap.summary: Denmark caps pouches at 9 mg/pouch and allows tobacco/menthol flavours only — delisting XQS's flavour-led, higher-strength SKUs in STG's home market. Unlike the France ban this is a restriction, so some volume recaptures into compliant SKUs (XQS has hedged into mint) — the recapture rate is the big unknown and drives the band. Modeled on STG's published pouch base; Denmark's pouch share and the delisted-SKU share are editable assumptions, not STG figures.
dk-cap.exposedBaseNote: NGP (nicotine-pouch) net sales FY2025 — STG's published pouch base (incl. XQS). STG does not split it by market, so Denmark's share is the editable assumption below.

assumptions.label: Denmark share of STG's pouch (NGP) base
assumptions.note: STG does not publish DK pouch revenue — you set it. Denmark is the home market but small (2.5% of group across all products).

assumptions.label: Delisted share (flavoured / >9 mg SKUs)
assumptions.note: SKU-grounded (Pouch Radar): a tobacco/menthol-only rule delists 23 of 34 UK and 27 of 34 SE XQS nicotine SKUs (68% / 79%) — 0.65 is the conservative anchor inside that band. Measured against XQS's full SE/UK range, not Denmark's already-mint-trimmed shelf (only 12% there — the survivors). SKU counts, not revenue (mint over-indexes on volume) — still editable.

assumptions.label: Volume recaptured into compliant SKUs
assumptions.note: Customers switching to compliant ≤9 mg tobacco/menthol SKUs (XQS hedged into mint). The biggest unknown — drives the band.

assumptions.label: Contribution margin on lost pouch revenue
assumptions.note: STG does not disclose pouch contribution margin (NGP gross margin was 35.2% in Q1 2026, but gross ≠ contribution) — an assumption. Drives the band.

facts.claim: NGP (nicotine-pouch) net sales — the pouch base (FY2025)

abstain.reason: not published by STG — you set it via 'Denmark share' (an analyst assumption, never STG's figure)
not stated in source — needs human lookup
no public consensus for pouches — contribution margin is an editable assumption, not a cited figure
